_doc: Reviewer sign-off on cross-artifact entity-ID alignment findings (SC-0018, the analogue of coverage-exceptions.json / lint-exceptions.json). A finding whose keys match an entry here is downgraded by `tripod id-check` from ERROR/MISALIGN/FLAG to ✓ ACCEPTED: it still appears in the inventory (marked accepted, with the reason), but is excluded from the failure count and does not fail the run. It exists for the legitimate cases — e.g. a map that declares an off-stage referent the MEANING_COORDINATES omits (already documented as a coverage difference by `tripod gold-diff`), or a deliberate surface variant — ruled by the project lead, NOT silently suppressed. The engine stays pure (it always surfaces the finding); acceptance is applied as a recorded downgrade, exactly as `tripod coverage` applies coverage-exceptions. Governed: every entry carries provenance (accepted_by/on + sc_ref); pinned in _spec/pins.json, so accepting an exception is a recorded, re-pinned edit. Match keys: pericope = map basename stem (e.g. 'P01-Ruth-1-1-5'); kind ∈ {REFERENCE_INTEGRITY, NAME_BINDING, MISALIGNMENT, FLAG_MISMATCH, DANGLING_NOTE}; then code (entity/flag findings) | note_title (dangling) | (scope, direction) also for MISALIGNMENT/FLAG_MISMATCH. SC-0020 adds the first signed-off entries: the ruled place/time coverage-difference one-siders (off-stage/contextual referents the MEANING_COORDINATES carries structurally but the map declares only contextually, or vice-versa — already-documented MM↔gold coverage differences).

exceptions.note: The covenant land they are returning *to* — referenced, not scene-present in the map's §3B. The MEANING_COORDINATES carries it as a structural place; a legitimate map↔MC coverage difference.
The covenant land they are returning *to* — referenced, not scene-present in the map's §3B. The MEANING_COORDINATES carries it as a structural place; a legitimate map↔MC coverage difference.
The harvest frame named at the close; MEANING_COORDINATES-structural only. A legitimate map↔MC coverage difference.
The gleaning locus in Boaz's command; MEANING_COORDINATES-structural only. A legitimate map↔MC coverage difference.
The already-documented MM↔gold coverage difference (the P04 95% gold-diff): the map declares PL1, the gold MEANING_COORDINATES omits it.
The already-documented MM↔gold coverage difference (the P04 95% gold-diff): the map declares PL2, the gold MEANING_COORDINATES omits it.

The field Ruth proposes to glean in. The MEANING_COORDINATES declares PL5 in S2 (Ruth's gleaning initiative); the map's S2 names it only generically ('the field', untagged) and reserves the [[PL5-Field]] wikilink for S3, where Ruth actually arrives. After the SC-0020 parity bar, this is not resolved by prose (the map references PL5 only in S3, not S2) — a legitimate cross-scene coverage difference, not a code disagreement.

Elimelech as clan-eponym. The MEANING_COORDINATES carries B2 in S3 only as the `clan_eponym` slot value behind the restated clan-frame; the map's S3 names the clan as [[B29-The-Clan-of-Elimelech]] and references B2 itself only in S1's introduction. After the SC-0020 parity bar this is not prose-resolved (no [[B2]] wikilink in S3) — a legitimate MC-slot-only coverage difference.

The within-day duration 'from morning until now' in the foreman's report. The MEANING_COORDINATES carries it as a structural §3C object; the map records the same content in its S4 §3C block as bare Hebrew+gloss text (no [[TH_…]] wikilink, following the P01 about-ten-years precedent that this is content, not a scene-setting frame). The map genuinely never wikilinks the code, so the parity bar leaves it — an MC-structural-only coverage difference; remains UNVERIFIABLE (no TH_ registry), which is acceptable.
